:::{#exm-fubini-fail-tonelli}

#### When neither Fubini–Tonelli condition is satisfied

The same function $f(x, y) = (x^2 - y^2)/(x^2 + y^2)^2$ from
@exm-fubini-fail illustrates a case where neither condition of
@thm-fubini-tonelli is satisfied.

**Why @thm-fubini-tonelli's hypothesis fails:**
$\iint_R |f|\,dA = \infty$, which violates hypothesis (b).
Switching to polar coordinates $(r, \theta)$ near the origin, the
integrand satisfies
$|f(x, y)| = \abs{x^2 - y^2}/(x^2 + y^2)^2 = \abs{\cos 2\theta}/r^2$,
so

$$
\ba
\iint_R |f|\,dA
&\ge \int_0^{\pi/2}\!\int_0^{\epsilon}
   \frac{\abs{\cos 2\theta}}{r^2}\, r\,dr\,d\theta\\
&= \paren{\int_0^{\pi/2}\abs{\cos 2\theta}\,d\theta}
       \int_0^{\epsilon} \frac{dr}{r}\\
&= +\infty,
\ea
$$

since $\int_0^{\epsilon} dr/r$ diverges.
Therefore $\iint_R |f|\,dA = \infty$,
and hypothesis (b) of @thm-fubini-tonelli is not satisfied.
(Hypothesis (a) also fails: $f$ takes both positive and negative values,
so it is not nonnegative a.e.)
The unequal iterated integrals from @exm-fubini-fail are thus consistent
with @thm-fubini-tonelli: the theorem simply does not apply.

[@wp:fubini]

:::
